---
title: "ML Course — Report Template"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: true
    self-contained: true
    theme: cosmo
    code-fold: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
jupyter: python3
bibliography: references.bib
---

## Introduction

Replace this section with your analysis. This template demonstrates the basic structure of a Quarto report.

Linear regression is a foundational method in supervised learning [@hastie2009].
@hastie2009 provide a comprehensive treatment of regularization and model selection.

## Loading Data

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
n = 100
x = rng.uniform(0, 10, n)
y = 2 * x + rng.normal(0, 2, n)

df = pd.DataFrame({"x": x, "y": y})
df.describe().round(2)
```

## Visualization

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))
ax.scatter(df["x"], df["y"], alpha=0.6)
ax.set(xlabel="x", ylabel="y", title="Simulated data")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Model

```{python}
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression

model = LinearRegression().fit(df[["x"]], df["y"])
print(f"Intercept: {model.intercept_:.2f}")
print(f"Slope:     {model.coef_[0]:.2f}")
```

## Conclusion

Summarize your findings here.

## References
